name: Rep. Jimmy Panetta
tweet: I have, and will continue to fight the President & his administration. I am fighting to increase access to affordable health care, protect our environment, invest in education, make housing more affordable, and more. I will continue to promote our Central Coast values.

name: Rep. Jimmy Panetta
tweet: The President has focused on slashing critical programs, instead of investing in issues impacting communities across the Central Coast and around the country: aging infrastructure, skyrocketing health care costs, widening student achievement gaps, and the climate crisis.

name: Rep. Jimmy Panetta
tweet: Cuts Social Security and other disability programs: Plans to restructure and reduce federal disability benefits, including the SSDI program. Altogether, the budget’s policies cut Social Security benefits by $24 billion – despite his claims he would leave Social Security alone

name: Rep. Jimmy Panetta
tweet: Makes steep and destructive cuts to health care, including to Medicaid, Medicare, and the Children's Health Insurance Program.

name: Rep. Jimmy Panetta
tweet: Limits lifesaving medical research: The budget cuts lifesaving medical research programs at the National Institutes of Health by $3.3 billion, and reduces funding for the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention by $678 million.

name: Rep. Jimmy Panetta
tweet: It also cuts the EPA by 26 percent – as the Administration continues recklessly to dismantle the protections that keep our air and water clean and safe, ignore the dangers of climate change, and put the profits of polluters ahead of the American people.

name: Rep. Jimmy Panetta
tweet: Slashes environmental protection and clean energy innovation: The budget lays bare his disdain for clean energy innovation and the health of our environment. It eliminates clean energy tax credits and advanced technology loan programs.

name: Rep. Jimmy Panetta
tweet: Disinvests in America’s students: The budget slashes resources for the Department of Education, makes huge cuts to student loan programs over 10 years, eliminates subsidized student loans, and eliminates the Public Service Loan Forgiveness program.

name: Rep. Jimmy Panetta
tweet: Neglects to make meaningful investments in infrastructure: The budget cuts funding for the Department of Transportation by 13 percent, including critical highway repair programs.

name: Rep. Jimmy Panetta
tweet: Further, the budget provides no new funding to the Housing Trust Fund, choking off resources that help states reduce costs for families struggling to afford housing. These are programs we use and need to make housing more affordable on the Central Coast.

name: Rep. Jimmy Panetta
tweet: Eliminates Housing Programs: The budget eliminates the Community Development Block Grant program and the HOME Investment Partnerships Program, a combined $4.8 billion cut that hinders communities’ housing rehabilitation and economic development opportunities.

name: Rep. Jimmy Panetta
tweet: Hurts Hungry Families: The budget cuts the Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (known as CalFresh). This would hurt the over 74,000 people on the Central Coast who need CalFresh to put food on the table.

name: Rep. Jimmy Panetta
tweet: Instead of prioritizing policies to strengthen our national security & bolster American leadership &values, the President once again proposes wasting billions of taxpayer dollars on an unnecessary and divisive border wall. Here are just some of the cuts he’s proposed. His budget:

name: Rep. Jimmy Panetta
tweet: Long thread: The President’s budget for FY2021 came out today. It’s reckless and callous. The proposals would gut investments in nutrition benefits, housing programs, climate science, and education on the Central Coast and across the country.

name: Rep. Jimmy Panetta
tweet: Watch now: From K-12 education to veterans’ health care, our budgets should support programs that enhance Americans’ daily lives.  Congress must pass the Investing for the People Act to protect these critical investments.

name: Rep. Jimmy Panetta
tweet: Congress owes the American people a budget that works. The Investing for the People Act sets forth realistic, two-year funding levels that make critical investments in our economic and national security.  https://t.co/nnu2fWXWj3

name: Rep. Jimmy Panetta
tweet: The House overwhelmingly passed my bill, the #NATOSupportAct, on a bipartisan 357 – 22 vote. Congress must ensure that America will continue to hold a strategic advantage over our adversaries both now and into the future. To do so, we must enshrine our commitment to #NATO in law

name: Rep. Jimmy Panetta
tweet: However, what must be made clear is that membership in NATO is not membership in a country club. It is a solemn commitment to the defense of the world order.  For it to succeed for another 70 years, the United States must continue to invest in the alliance.

name: Rep. Jimmy Panetta
tweet: #NATOSupportAct affirms that our NATO partners must pay at least 2 percent of their GDP on defense and promotes robust funding for the European Deterrence Initiative to counter Russian aggression.

name: Rep. Jimmy Panetta
tweet: Today, in the face of Russian threats and attacks on American and allied interests, the U.S. must take a stand and solidify our commitment to our allies. We must live up to the #NATO pledge that an attack on one of us is an attack on all of us. #NATOSupportAct

name: Rep. Jimmy Panetta
tweet: I left that country in 2008, but after 17 years of our engagement there, NATO troops are still serving alongside our men and women in uniform.

name: Rep. Jimmy Panetta
tweet: We continue to see #NATO’s value in our enduring fight against terrorism. In the hours after the 9/11 attacks on our soil, our NATO partners volunteered to invoke Article 5. I served alongside many troops from many NATO countries during my service in Afghanistan.

name: Rep. Jimmy Panetta
tweet: Established in an effort to contain Soviet expansionism after World War II, #NATO has preserved peace, fostered economic prosperity, and promoted democratic governance. The mutual defense pledge is why NATO has been the most successful alliance in history.

name: Rep. Jimmy Panetta
tweet: I look forward to hearing #NATO Secretary General Stoltenberg’s address to a joint session of Congress. The House recently voiced its strong support for keeping the U.S. in the alliance when they voted overwhelmingly for my bipartisan bill the #NATOSupportAct.

name: Rep. Jimmy Panetta
tweet: This week marks the 70th anniversary of #NATO. For it to succeed for the next 70 years and beyond, we must continue to invest in this vital alliance. #NATOSupportAct
